result:
Build is green. Report:

**Root cause:** `lua_resume` at `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:1329` converted `Err(e)` from `raw_run_protected` into a `LuaStatus` but did NOT push the carried error value onto the stack. C-Lua's longjmp-based error path always leaves the error message at `L->top - 1`, which is where `set_error_obj` (line 165) reads it. Without the push, `set_error_obj` for `ErrRun` picked up whatever happened to be on top — in this case `Int(10)`, the leftover yield argument from the failed `coroutine.yield(10)` call inside `dofile`'s non-yieldable `state.call`.

**Fix:** In `lua_resume`'s `Err(e) => ...` arm, push `e.into_value()` onto the stack before returning the status (skipping the push when status is `Yield`, since yield args are already on top). This mirrors the same pattern already present in `pcall` (`do_.rs:1540`).

**New state after fix:** The test now correctly fails with `Runtime: attempt to yield across a C-call boundary` — the genuine error message the test was trying to produce. The deeper underlying issue (the `dofile` test on line 193-205 of files.lua wants the `lua_callk` continuation machinery, which is Phase E coroutine work) is now visible and unmasked. Goalpost moved past `Int(10)` confusion.

tool_input.command: cargo build -q -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -cE "^error" ; echo build-exit=$?
